title: What I think about Capital Punishment

transcript.text: Hello, my name is Kyle Pounds, and this is my video, What I Think About Capital Punishment.
I am against capital punishment. I think it's bad.
As a country, we should be striving to express the highest forms of life.
Trying to emulate Jesus and God.
And God would not kill.
Jesus would not kill.
We respect life. Life comes in here and we keep it alive.
And we let God take it away when it's time.
It's barbaric.
And it's also very expensive.
With all the appeals that people go through, many years,
It's a lot cheaper to just let them stay in jail.
Another reason, let's see here, I have a little list here.
It does break the Cruel and Unusual Punishment Clause in the Bill of Rights.
So it's unconstitutional.
That is Cruel and Unusual Punishment.
Most of us are afraid to die, even crazy criminals.
Well, and also, I think it's painful.
Let's see here.
Yeah, we as a society should move away from an eye-for-an-eye revenge mentality if civilization is too advanced.
I think I wrote that.
Yeah, that revenge.
Capital punishment is kind of like revenge.
It's one of the arguments that people are for capital punishment have.
It gives closure to the victim's family who've suffered so much.
That's just appealing to the bloodthirsty side of us.
And I don't think it's punishing the person or helping the person to deal with themselves anymore.
Or they'll die and then they'll just be happy dead and get reborn again and probably not the greatest life.
But if you put somebody in jail for the rest of their life, not for killing somebody, instead of killing them,
They'll have their whole life to sit around and think about what they did.
If you kill them, they'll forget what they did and they'll keep doing it.
But if you don't kill them, you let them stay alive and sit in a jail cell the rest of their life,
They'll think about it and then they won't do it again.
It's better recovery for them to sit in jail.
And let's see what's another.
It provides a deterrent for prisoners already serving a life sentence.
Like they won't kill another prisoner if they're afraid that they'll get the death penalty.
There's plenty of ways to punish prisoners in prison.
You could just give them solitary confinement for the rest of their life.
And let's see here.
Prisoner parole or escapees can give criminals another chance to kill.
People don't escape jail.
And if they do, then it's the jail's fault.
You can design jails if criminals can't escape.
And parole, well, that's up to the court system.
You're kind of judging the court system without any reasons.
You have to treat it like a case-by-case scenario.
So, I'm against capital punishment.
It's too expensive.
Our court system is clogged enough.
Just throw them in jail.
And there's always a chance, you know,
That the person, it turned out the person's not guilty or something.
And I think if they live in jail, we can put them to work.
There's all sorts of stuff they could do.
Manual labor.
I think we should turn all jails into factories.
Have these people show up, do their job,
And go home to their cell.
So, that'll be great for the economy.
And as far as the prison system,
That's a whole other conversation.
So, have a nice day.
